Article.ArticleTitle: MR-Guided Laser Interstitial Thermal Therapy for Recurrent Glioblastoma: A Case Report With Novel Insights Into Histopathological Changes and Immunological Responses.

Abstract.AbstractText: MR-guided laser interstitial thermal therapy (LITT) offers a minimally invasive option for recurrent glioblastoma, but human post-treatment tissue data are scarce. In this case report, post-LITT tissue exhibited distinct zonal changes, including central necrosis within the ablation zone and reduced tumour proliferation with increased CD8+ T-cell infiltration in the perilesional transition zone, suggesting that LITT may influence the local immune landscape in recurrent glioblastoma.
Abstract.CopyrightInformation: © 2026 The Author(s). Neuropathology and Applied Neurobiology published by John Wiley & Sons Ltd on behalf of British Neuropathological Society.

MedlineCitation.CoiStatement: Silas Haahr Nielsen and Rune Rasmussen have received travel grants from Medtronic for attending conferences. Additionally, Medtronic has co‐sponsored a conference at which these authors served as hosts.

Reference.Citation: Schulze P. C., Vitzthum H. E., Goldammer A., Schneider J. P., and Schober R., “Laser‐Induced Thermotherapy of Neoplastic Lesions in the Brain? Underlying Tissue Alterations, MRI‐Monitoring and Clinical Applicability,” Acta Neurochirurgica 146, no. 8 (2004): 803–812, 10.1007/s00701-004-0293-5.

Tracz R. A., Wyman D. R., Little P. B., et al., “Comparison of Magnetic Resonance Images and the Histopathological Findings of Lesions Induced by Interstitial Laser Photocoagulation in the Brain,” Lasers in Surgery and Medicine 13, no. 1 (1993): 45–54, 10.1002/lsm.1900130110.

Chandar J. S., Bhatia S., Ingle S., et al., “Laser Interstitial Thermal Therapy Induces Robust Local Immune Response for Newly Diagnosed Glioblastoma With Long‐Term Survival and Disease Control,” Journal of Immunotherapy 46, no. 9 (2023): 351–354, 10.1097/CJI.0000000000000485.

Elder J. B., Huntoon K., Otero J., et al., “Histologic Findings Associated With Laser Interstitial Thermotherapy for Glioblastoma Multiforme,” Diagnostic Pathology 14, no. 1 (2019): 19, 10.1186/s13000-019-0794-4.
Cruz J. V. R., Batista C., Afonso B. d. H., et al., “Obstacles to Glioblastoma Treatment Two Decades After Temozolomide,” Cancers 14, no. 13 (2022): 3203, 10.3390/cancers14133203.

Himes B. T., Geiger P. A., Ayasoufi K., Bhargav A. G., Brown D. A., and Parney I. F., “Immunosuppression in Glioblastoma: Current Understanding and Therapeutic Implications,” Frontiers in Oncology 11 (2021): 770561, 10.3389/fonc.2021.770561.
Nielsen S. H. and Rasmussen R., “MR‐Guided Laser Interstitial Thermal Therapy in the Treatment of Brain Tumors and Epilepsy,” Acta Neurochirurgica 166, no. 1 (2024): 344, 10.1007/s00701-024-06238-0.
O'Halloran P. J., Henry J., Amoo M., et al., “LITTing up Gliomas—Is the Future Bright?” World Neurosurgery 17 (2023): 100136, 10.1016/j.wnsx.2022.100136.

Nielsen S. H., Skjøth‐Rasmussen J., Larsen V. A., et al., “Blood‐Brain Barrier Disruption Following MR‐Guided Laser Interstitial Thermal Therapy,” Neuro‐Oncology Advances 7 (2025): vdaf148, 10.1093/noajnl/vdaf148.

Shin D. H., Melnick K. F., Tran D. D., and Ghiaseddin A. P., “In Situ Vaccination With Laser Interstitial Thermal Therapy Augments Immunotherapy in Malignant Gliomas,” Journal of Neuro‐Oncology 151, no. 1 (2021): 85–92, 10.1007/s11060-020-03557-x.

Stupp R., Mason W. P., van den Bent M. J., et al., “Radiotherapy Plus Concomitant and Adjuvant Temozolomide for Glioblastoma,” New England Journal of Medicine 352, no. 10 (2005): 987–996, 10.1056/NEJMoa043330.

Müller K. J., Forbrig R., Reis J., et al., “Measurable Disease as Baseline Criterion for Response Assessment in Glioblastoma: A Comparison of PET ‐Based (PET RANO 1.0) and MRI‐Based (RANO) Assessments,” Neuro‐Oncology 27, no. 1 (2025): 77–88, 10.1093/neuonc/noae208.

Menovsky T., Beek J. F., van Gemert M. J. C., Roux F. X., and Bown S. G., “Interstitial Laser Thermotherapy in Neurosurgery: A Review,” Acta Neurochirurgica 138, no. 9 (1996): 1019–1026, 10.1007/BF01412303.

Artzi S. B., Klausen M. N., Harwood D. S. L., et al., “Spatial Transcriptomic Analysis Reveals Lack of Response to PD‐1 Blockade in Recurrent Glioblastoma,” Acta Neuropathologica 150, no. 1 (2025): 29, 10.1007/s00401-025-02937-9.

Knudsen A. M., Ewald J. D., Pedersen V., Haupt‐Jorgensen M., Hansen E. V. R., and Kristensen B. W., “Characterisation of the Tumour Microenvironment in Primary and Recurrent Glioblastomas,” Neuropathology and Applied Neurobiology 50, no. 5 (2024): e13012, 10.1111/nan.13012.

Knudsen A. M., Halle B., Cédile O., et al., “Surgical Resection of Glioblastomas Induces Pleiotrophin‐Mediated Self‐Renewal of Glioblastoma Stem Cells in Recurrent Tumors,” Neuro‐Oncology 24, no. 7 (2022): 1074–1087, 10.1093/neuonc/noab302.
